title: OpenCode Subagent Handoff Contract
description: Structured payload that @orchestrator or @artifact-planner must send to a worker lane (@fixer, @frontend, @backend, @mobile, @devops, @designer, etc.) for non-trivial work. Plans without valid handoff blocks for non-trivial worklist items are not execution-ready.

task_id.description: Stable identifier shared with the plan and the runtime run, e.g. landing-2026-06-30.

plan_id.description: Plan id or plan filename stem; usually identical to task_id.

caller.description: Lane that originates the delegation. Must be one of orchestrator or artifact-planner in practice, but the schema does not enforce a fixed set so worker-only validation is the responsibility of the orchestrator tooling.

callee.description: Worker lane that should execute. The validator (subagent-handoff-check.py) enforces membership in the 22-lane allowlist independently of this schema.

scope.description: Single concrete outcome the worker owns. Avoid compound sentences.

claim_level.description: Maximum completion claim the worker is allowed to make. 'done' means production-ready; lower levels are non-blocking drafts.

claim_scope.description: Free-form clarification of what is in scope and what is NOT in scope for the claim.

source_basis.description: Files, paths, docs, or references the worker must treat as authority.

must_preserve.description: Invariants that cannot be broken even if a shortcut seems easier.

do_not_touch.description: Paths or scopes that are out of bounds. Path traversal segments ('..') are rejected by the validator.

validation.description: Commands or checks the worker must run before reporting done.

evidence_required.description: Required evidence paths (logs, screenshots, manifests, contract files).

depends_on.description: Task ids or named prerequisites; the worker should not start until these are complete.

context_bundle.description: Files the worker should inspect first to ground the task without rediscovering the repo.

examples.scope: Implement landing hero + 3 feature sections + CTA
examples.claim_scope: hero + features + CTA only; no testimonials, no pricing
